dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal requires visual chart spike detection ('CRB chart shows visually obvious spike') which cannot be quantified with allowed indicators. Approximated spike detection using 5-day returns >20% combined with elevated 20-day historical volatility as proxy for hysteria/exhaustion. Entry triggers on 1st pullback (opposite direction move) or spike day itself. Exit conditions map to: prior extreme break (3% threshold on reversal), 15-day time stop, and 1.5:1 profit target (approximated as 1.5% return given 2.5% position size). Position direction inferred from market condition (long on downspike, short on upspike).

backtest_rule.reason: Signal requires visual chart spike detection ('CRB chart shows visually obvious spike') which cannot be quantified with allowed indicators. Approximated spike detection using 5-day returns >20% combined with elevated 20-day historical volatility as proxy for hysteria/exhaustion. Entry triggers on 1st pullback (opposite direction move) or spike day itself. Exit conditions map to: prior extreme break (3% threshold on reversal), 15-day time stop, and 1.5:1 profit target (approximated as 1.5% return given 2.5% position size). Position direction inferred from market condition (long on downspike, short on upspike).

source_rule_text: A trader should take contrarian positions against extreme price moves (hysteria) when charts show incredible spikes, because exhaustion patterns often precede reversions.
